personas.name: The Pragmatic Optimizer
key_characteristics: Highly systematic approach to food and delivery decisions
Views delivery as a calculated convenience tool rather than indulgence
Develops structured routines and optimization systems
delivery_triggers: Strategic use of promotions and deals
pain_points: Hidden fees and price inflation on apps
Minimum order requirements for solo ordering
deliveroo_opportunities: Transparent pricing with no hidden fees
Loyalty points convertible to future orders

evidence.quote: Then again, like I said with ordering a takeaway after, you know, sort of after an exam or something like that, that's, that's an enjoyable meal. It's like a reward. You've earned it.
Well, obviously a lot of it just comes down to like price and making things cheaper. So if they're going to make it more for students, obviously reducing the price would be one.
I'd say delivery fee will be super important for students. The speed as well.

personas.name: The Social Orderer
key_characteristics: Delivery becomes acceptable and less guilt-inducing in group contexts
Social influence heavily impacts ordering decisions
Friday evenings and weekend socializing drive orders
Collaborative price comparison and platform choice
pain_points: Minimum order thresholds for group sizes
deliveroo_opportunities: Group ordering features with automatic payment splitting

evidence.quote: I guess, like, getting takeaway with friends or, like, I feel like when you're going out with friends, it's not as... like, it doesn't feel as bad getting a pizza or getting a spice bag. And, like, it definitely feels more like a social thing, being able to... like, rather than, like, ordering it to your house and, like, eating it on your own.

Anything with like, like as I said, like hanging out with my friends when we have a dinner plan or anything like that. Like I just came back from spoons with my friends so I think that's the thing. Like that feels social and comfortable for me.

I sat next to my mate, and he was... We were talking about what we was... get tweaks. There's a shop right next to the bus stop, and he was like, 'Oh, I just think I'm going to order something.' And then that's all, like, planted a seed in my head.

personas.name: The Convenience Dependent
key_characteristics: High frequency ordering (multiple times per week)
Delivery integrated into daily routine as necessity
Time constraints and busy schedules drive dependence
Subscription services to optimize frequent usage
delivery_triggers: Lack of transportation or mobility constraints
pain_points: High cumulative costs from frequent ordering
Delivery address complications in student housing
deliveroo_opportunities: Bulk subscription discounts for frequent users

evidence.quote: Okay, so pretty much it was in the morning, and I started noticing that we're running out of cereal, eggs, milk, porridge, and all that stuff. I was like, I don't feel like going down to the shop... and I didn't have the time to go down to the shop.

So this is a very, like, funny, small thing, but it's kind of difficult getting the deliveries, like, actually where you want them. So maybe it's just, like, the setup here at Oxford and the different colleges
I mean... definitely winter. I think that's probably what started it... For me... just not wanting to leave the house as much.

personas.name: The Budget Balancer
personas.tagline: Careful financial management meets occasional treats
key_characteristics: Strict budget consciousness with delivery as calculated treat
Strong preference for cooking and meal prep
Delivery reserved for special occasions or emergencies
delivery_triggers: Emergency situations when cooking isn't possible
pain_points: High delivery costs relative to home cooking
Limited budget flexibility for spontaneous orders
deliveroo_opportunities: Emergency meal solutions at lower cost
Cooking ingredient delivery for meal prep
Occasional treat positioning rather than routine convenience

evidence.quote: justified is probably just when personally, for me, it's like, if it's Been a while if it's something that I've been feeling like having and if I like if it's time to do another grocery shop
I'm not really the kind of person that likes having subscriptions anyway because I just don't... like, like money coming out. Like, I prefer to pay for things, like, up front
I think... I think establishing more of those kind of, like, set-in-stone discounts for certain restaurants
